# External Writing Resources

Curated free, legally redistributable writing craft resources bundled for the Plotzy bilingual writing course.
Every file below is either public domain (published before 1929, or by an author who died centuries ago) or released under an explicit Creative Commons license. Nothing here is paywalled, email gated, or All Rights Reserved.

| filename | title | author | language | license (exact) | source URL | why it helps a writer |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| poetics-aristotle-butcher.pdf | The Poetics of Aristotle | Aristotle, translated by S. H. Butcher | English | Public domain (published 1895, US public domain) | https://archive.org/details/poeticstranslate00arisuoft | The founding text on plot, character, and dramatic structure that every story theory still builds on. |
| the-art-of-fiction-james.pdf | The Art of Fiction | Henry James (with Walter Besant) | English | Public domain (published 1884, US public domain) | https://archive.org/details/artfiction00jamegoog | A landmark essay arguing that fiction is a serious craft, on selection, point of view, and lived detail. |
| craft-of-fiction-lubbock.pdf | The Craft of Fiction | Percy Lubbock | English | Public domain (published 1921, US public domain) | https://archive.org/details/craftoffiction030210mbp | A close, practical study of viewpoint and scene versus summary using the great novels as models. |
| a-study-of-prose-fiction-perry.pdf | A Study of Prose Fiction | Bliss Perry | English | Public domain (published 1902, US public domain) | https://archive.org/details/studyofprosefict00perr | A clear survey of plot, character, setting, and theme, useful as a beginner's map of narrative technique. |
| technique-of-the-mystery-story-wells.pdf | The Technique of the Mystery Story | Carolyn Wells | English | Public domain (published 1913, US public domain) | https://archive.org/details/cu31924027194905 | A practical manual on suspense, clues, fair play, and plotting for genre and mystery writers. |
| elements-of-style-strunk.pdf | The Elements of Style | William Strunk Jr. | English | Public domain (published 1920, US public domain) | https://archive.org/details/cu31924014450716 | The classic short guide to clean, concise sentences and disciplined usage. |
| openstax-writing-guide-with-handbook.pdf | Writing Guide with Handbook | OpenStax (Michelle Bachelor Robinson, Maria Jerskey, and others) | English | Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International (CC BY 4.0) | https://openstax.org/details/books/writing-guide | A modern, genre based composition textbook covering the full writing process, revision, and a usage handbook. |
| al-umda-ibn-rashiq-1907.pdf | العمدة في صناعة الشعر ونقده (al-Umda fi Sinaʿat al-Shiʿr wa-Naqdih), volume 1 | ابن رشيق القيرواني (Ibn Rashiq al-Qayrawani) | Arabic | Public domain (1907 edition, author died 1064 CE) | https://archive.org/details/AAlexandrina-153227 | The foundational Arabic manual on the craft and criticism of poetry, imagery, meter, and rhetorical figures. |
| adab-al-katib-ibn-qutayba-1927.pdf | أدب الكاتب (Adab al-Katib) | ابن قتيبة الدينوري (Ibn Qutayba al-Dinawari) | Arabic | Public domain (1927 edition, author died 889 CE) | https://archive.org/details/AAlexandrina-195125 | The classic Arabic handbook for the prose writer on correct diction, style, and common errors of composition. |

## Notes on licensing decisions

- All English classics are pre 1929 publications, which places them in the US public domain.
- The two Arabic works are scans of pre 1929 print editions (1907 and 1927) of texts whose authors died roughly a thousand years ago, so both the text and these specific editions are public domain.
- OpenStax Writing Guide with Handbook is released under CC BY 4.0, which permits free redistribution and adaptation with attribution, including in a free course.

## Candidates rejected for licensing reasons

- Hindawi Foundation books (hindawi.org and its safahat.org mirror), including الفن الروائي by David Lodge and الحكاية وما فيها. Rejected because the Hindawi site is behind Cloudflare and returns HTTP 403 to automated download, and because many of these titles are offered free by private author agreement rather than under a redistributable license, so they fail the strict rule.
- Modern critical editions of Arabic classics on archive.org (for example a 1998 edition of البيان والتبيين, and editions of دلائل الإعجاز and أسرار البلاغة). Rejected because the underlying classical text is public domain but the modern editor's critical apparatus and typesetting carry a separate copyright, and the uploads had no license metadata.
- كتاب الصناعتين (al-Sinaʿatayn) by Abu Hilal al-Askari. Wanted for its relevance to prose and poetry craft, but the only scan found was a copyrighted 1971 Dar al-Fikr edition, so it was skipped.
- Elements of Creative Writing (University of Northern Iowa, CC BY-NC 4.0). The license is fine for a free course, but its ScholarWorks or bepress host blocks automated download with an Incapsula challenge, so the file could not be retrieved cleanly. OpenStax Writing Guide (CC BY 4.0) was used as the modern open textbook instead.
- Controlled digital lending scans on archive.org (identifiers ending in 0000, for example techniqueofmyste0000unse). Rejected because they are borrow only and often still in copyright.
